summary: Added US-844-scoped integration coverage in src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts. The new tests drive real engine settlement transitions into renderer assertions: one proves settleDeal carries bullet and mutual-whist updates into next-deal score/result UI, and one proves a completed bullet shows the final ranking/result panel with no continuation controls. The work stayed inside integration tests and task reporting; production domain, UI, and persistence code were not changed.

tests_run.evidence: Red-green cycle completed: the new US-844 integration file failed first on settlement/result UI assumptions, then passed with 2 tests after fixture tightening.

tests_run.command: npm run test -- src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts src/test/startResume.integration.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts
tests_run.evidence: 11 integration tests passed across rule flow, start/resume, and the new US-844 scoring coverage.
26 UI/controller tests passed, guarding renderer behavior touched by the new integration assertions.
30 persistence tests passed, providing compatibility evidence for score/result state and finished-bullet resume suppression.
Vitest: 13 files, 125 tests passed. Build: tsc and vite build succeeded.

downstream_notes: Persistence compatibility evidence came from the existing saveStore suite because this task did not need to modify persistence behavior or schema validation.
The new integration coverage intentionally uses deterministic hand-written end-of-deal fixtures instead of a long random full-bullet flow to keep US-844 failure signals specific and reproducible.

verification_environment.verification_scope: focused integration/UI/persistence verification followed by full npm run verify

node_package_scripts.verify: npm run test && npm run build

verification_environment.notes: The first npm run verify attempt failed in TypeScript build because a temporary test helper used loosely typed card literals. The helper was replaced with real typed deck cards and npm run verify was rerun to green.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Maintain bullet, mountain, and mutual-whist values for all players after a scored deal settles.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts src/test/startResume.integration.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts
evidence_matrix.limitations: Coverage is deterministic in-process integration over engine plus renderer, not a browser-automation or DOS-binary equivalence proof.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Expose each deal result and the final completed-bullet settlement to the UI.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; src/ui/render.test.ts; npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts; npm run verify
evidence_matrix.limitations: UI evidence is DOM-level integration/component coverage rather than Playwright visual artifacts for this task.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Given a deal completes, when the deal result is settled, the score table shows updated bullet, mountain, and mutual whist values for all players.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts first scenario; npm run test -- src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; npm run verify
evidence_matrix.limitations: Assertions validate rendered text/regions and settled score objects in jsdom rather than screenshot comparison.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Given the bullet target is reached, when final settlement is calculated, the game shows the final ranking/result summary and prevents accidental continuation.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts second scenario; src/test/startResume.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts src/test/startResume.integration.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/persistence/saveStore.test.ts; npm run verify
evidence_matrix.limitations: Continuation prevention is covered via absence of finished-state action controls and existing saved-bullet resume suppression tests, not by new browser E2E automation.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Tie scoring, rule flow, and UI together in one deterministic integration layer without changing production domain/UI code.
evidence_matrix.limitations: This contract is satisfied by scoped source changes and verification evidence rather than a separate runtime output.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: Red-green cycle on npm run test -- src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; deterministic fixture scenarios in src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Representative deterministic settlement fixtures are narrower than a full bullet-play matrix.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: npm run test -- src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts src/test/startResume.integration.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts; npm run verify
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Regression evidence is local deterministic test/build coverage.
No new persistence schema or save/load behavior was introduced in this task; this is compatibility confirmation only.
Release evidence is scoped to the repo's existing static-app test/build entrypoints.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; src/ui/render.test.ts; npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: This task used DOM-level semantic and control-presence assertions, not external accessibility tooling or visual screenshot diffing.
Task scope is local integration tests only, with no auth, backend, network, or sensitive-data path.
